alchemy-missing-warning: The Alchemy mod is required for crafting
botaniastory:handbook-intro-text: Welcome to the world of floral magic! Botania Story is a mod that adds many new mechanics related to mystical flora and mana management. To begin your journey, you will need the main tool of every botanist — the book of knowledge. You can view its crafting recipe by clicking this link: Botania Lexicon. Craft it, hold it in your hands, and right-click to immerse yourself in magic!
botaniastory:talisman-hint-bind: Shift + Right-click on a block — bind block type.
botaniastory:talisman-hint-drop: Ctrl + Shift + Right-click — place the talisman.
botaniastory:talisman-hint-dump: Shift + Right-click on a chest — dump blocks.
botaniastory:talisman-hint-toggle: Shift + Right-click in the air — toggle absorption.
error-stove-invalid: The stove structure is incomplete or invalid!
flowerbag-hint-dump: Shift + RMB on chest: Dump flowers
flowerbag-hint-toggle: Shift + RMB in air: Toggle auto-collect
itemdesc-pickaxe-terrashatterer-0-off: A legendary pickaxe capable of destroying enormous areas of land. This is the base class and can be upgraded by infusing it with mana.
itemdesc-pickaxe-terrashatterer-1-off: This item cannot be crafted in a crafting grid. It is obtained by infusing the base Terra Shatterer (Class 0) with mana.
itemdesc-pickaxe-terrashatterer-2-off: This item cannot be crafted in a crafting grid. It is obtained by infusing the Class 1 Terra Shatterer with mana.
itemdesc-pickaxe-terrashatterer-3-off: This item cannot be crafted in a crafting grid. It is obtained by infusing the Class 2 Terra Shatterer with mana.
itemdesc-pickaxe-terrashatterer-4-off: This item cannot be crafted in a crafting grid. It is obtained by infusing the Class 3 Terra Shatterer with mana.
itemdesc-pickaxe-terrashatterer-5-off: This item cannot be crafted in a crafting grid. It is obtained by infusing the Class 4 Terra Shatterer with mana. This is the maximum power level.
msg-debugger-copied: UI dictionary copied! Press Ctrl+V in code.
msg-debugger-saved: UI settings successfully saved to lexicon_client.json!
welcome-text:
Magic, Technology. Nature.

Botania is a tech mod centered around natural magic. Its core concept is creating magical flowers and devices that harness the power of the earth in the form of mana.
item-terrashatterer_parts: Golden handle parts of the Terra Shatterer
lexicon_basics_and_mechanics_apothecary_:
To create plants capable of carrying out various tasks, a botanist must fill them with mystical energy. Fortunately for you, the Petal Apothecary does exactly that.

Place it on the ground and fill it with clean water. By using <link=nothing>RMB</link>, you can place <link=mysticalflower>Mystical Petals</link> inside to release their energy. Once the required petals are inside, throw in <link=search:tree seeds>any tree seeds</link> to complete the process.

With the Petal Apothecary, you can create many plants, described in detail in the <link=category:functional_flora>Functional Flora</link> and <link=category:generating_flora>Generating Flora</link> sections.

By pressing <link=nothing>Shift+RMB</link> on the Apothecary with an empty hand, you will retrieve the last inserted item.














A simple tip for those who dislike routine work: within 20 seconds after crafting a flower in the Apothecary, you can press <link=nothing>RMB</link> on it with an empty hand to place into the Apothecary all
items from your inventory that were required for the previous recipe, which is useful for crafting large quantities of flowers. Works only if there is water inside.
lexicon_basics_and_mechanics_basicsintro: Welcome to the World of Botania!

Welcome!

Congratulations on opening this lexicon. Perhaps you found it in a chest, obtained it another way, or crafted it yourself. In any case, it will become your main assistant in learning Botania. You can turn pages using the arrows at the bottom of the page.

Botania is a mod about nature magic, but at its core it works as a system of mechanisms and processes. Here you will observe, experiment, and invent ways to interact with plants and mana. In time, you will be able to create useful devices, tools, and other unusual things.

This mod is suited for those who enjoy exploring systems and gradually building more complex constructions. Although Botania can function on its own, it also combines well with other mods. Many processes can be simplified and organized so they work almost automatically.

If you do not know where to begin, study the articles in the book and follow the natural progression. The lexicon contains many sections and useful hints, so it is worth spending some time getting familiar with its interface.

That concludes the introduction. Now you may proceed to study Botania and begin discovering its possibilities. I hope exploring this world of plants and mana will be just as interesting for you as creating it was for me.
lexicon_basics_and_mechanics_botanialexi:
The Botania Lexicon is a treasure trove of all knowledge for your botanical needs. But you probably already knew that. All information is found here. For convenience, if a block has a description, you can press <link=nothing>Shift+RMB</link> while holding the Botania Lexicon to open that description.

I highly recommend reading all entries in the <link=category:basics_and_mechanics>Basics and Mechanics</link> section before moving on to the others. Most sections contain one or more entries, so be sure to study them carefully.
lexicon_basics_and_mechanics_mysticalflo:
While exploring the world, you may come across different kinds of Mystical Flora.











      Wherever you look, flowers are everywhere...

As for mystical flowers, finding them will not take long, as they emit light. They come in 16 different colors.

Plucking petals from flowers.









These flowers can be used to obtain Mystical Petals, which are used to craft magical tools and plants.

These petals can also be ground into Mystical Pollen, which can be used as an ingredient in certain recipes, such as <link=floralfertilizer>"Mystical Flower Fertilizer"</link>. I also plan to add dye recipes later.
lexicon_basics_and_mechanics_puredaisy_p: <link=puredaisy>Pure Daisy</link> is not only the very first flower, but also one of the most important flowers for any botanist. This flower purifies and transforms adjacent wood and stone blocks into their purified forms: <link=livingwood>Livingwood</link> and <link=puredaisy>Livingrock</link>, both of which are needed for crafting many items.
Just give it a minute or so...
<link=livingwood_firepit>Livingwood Firewood</link> can in turn be processed into <link=livingwood_stick>Livingwood Sticks.</link>

Crafting the Pure Daisy.

















A blank sheet of paper for the beginning of your adventures.
lexicon_basics_and_mechanics_pylon_p1: <link=manapylon>Mana Pylon</link> is an ancient magical structure capable of drawing hidden power from the world and directing it into nearby mechanisms and artifacts. Its purpose will expand as the mod develops, unlocking new ways to work with mana. For now, it serves as the basis for creating more advanced structures — the <link=pylon>Nature Pylon</link> and the <link=gaiapylon>Gaia Pylon</link>.
lexicon_basics_and_mechanics_pylon_p2: <link=pylon>Nature Pylon</link> is filled with the energy of forests, flowers, and life itself. It is required to awaken and open the gateway to <link=manaitem>Alfheim</link> — the legendary realm of elves, where magic intertwines with nature.
lexicon_basics_and_mechanics_pylon_p3: <link=gaiapylon>Gaia Pylon</link> holds the power of the primal world and is an essential part of the ritual to summon Gaia. Only by placing such pylons and preparing the arena can one challenge the ancient force and test their skill.
lexicon_basics_and_mechanics_rune_p1:
<link=rune>Runes</link> are magical symbols containing the powers of nature, the seasons, the elements, and various aspects of the world. In Botania, they are used as important components for creating advanced devices, flowers, and artifacts. Each rune represents a specific concept, and their combinations allow mana to be directed and transformed into more complex forms.

There are 16 types of Runes in total.

The most basic elemental runes are named after the four elements. Mid-tier runes are named after the four seasons, while the most advanced runes are named after the seven deadly sins. The final rune on this list is the Rune of Mana.
lexicon_basics_and_mechanics_runicaltar_:
Creating <link=rune>runes</link> is an important step in the development of botanical magic. To learn how to create them, you first need a solid understanding of mana management. Before continuing, read the first articles in the <link=category:mana_management>Mana Management</link> section.

<link=rune>Runes</link> are extremely important components used in crafting advanced magical devices and flora. To make them, as you may have guessed, you need a Runic Altar. First place the items required for the desired <link=rune>rune</link> onto it by using <link=nothing>RMB</link> with them on the altar. Then direct a <link=manaspreader>Mana Spreader</link> at the altar to transfer some mana into it.

Crafting the Runic Altar.










The altar filling with mana can be observed while looking at it with the <link=wandoftheforest>Wand of the Forest</link> in hand. When the mana bar reaches more than half full, place <link=puredaisy>Livingrock</link> onto the altar, then press <link=nothing>RMB</link> on it with the <link=wandoftheforest>Wand of the Forest</link> to complete the creation of the <link=rune>rune</link>.
Pressing <link=nothing>Shift+RMB</link> on the Runic Altar with an empty hand will return the last item placed into it.

Any <link=rune>runes</link> used to craft something on the Runic Altar act as catalysts and are not consumed after crafting is complete. As with the Petal Apothecary, pressing RMB on the altar with an empty hand will place onto it the items used in the last recipe.
lexicon_basics_and_mechanics_terrasteel_: <link=terrasteel>Terrasteel</link> is a mighty metal infused with the power of nature. To create it, place a <link=game:ingot-steel>Steel Ingot</link>, <link=manapool>Mana Quartz</link>, and a <link=manapool>Mana Gear</link> on top of the completed platform. You will also need to aim a mana spreader at the center of the altar, or place a spark on the altar and power it using a <link=manapool>Mana Pool</link> with a <link=sparkaugment>Dominant Spark Augment</link>. Once infused with enough mana, the materials will merge into a single Terrasteel ingot.

Creating the Terrestrial Platform:

















1. <link=livingrock>Livingrock</link>
2. <link=game:metalblock-new-riveted-manasteel>Riveted Manasteel Block</link>
3. <link=terrestrialplate>Terrestrial Plate</link>
lexicon_basics_and_mechanics_wandofthefo:
The wand has several functions combined into one mode. While holding it, you can automatically see the amount of mana in magical items — simply look at an item containing mana.

The wand is also used for binding. To bind a Mana Spreader to a pool or another device, first press <link=nothing>RMB</link> on the spreader, then <link=nothing>RMB</link> on the object you wish to bind it to. If you want to bind a specific <link=category:generating_flora>flower</link> to a certain spreader, press <link=nothing>Shift+RMB</link> on the flower, then <link=nothing>Shift+RMB</link> on the desired spreader.
lexicon_elfmania_alfheimgates_p1:
Many, many years ago, <link=alfheim>elves</link> shared the world with Seraphim. However, for unknown reasons, they were driven back to their own realm — <link=alfheim>Alfheim</link>. Countless experiments were carried out in an attempt to bridge the gap between the two worlds, and in the end the means to create the gateway were found.

Constructing this gateway will not be an easy task, as it requires quite a number of rare resources. You will need 8 blocks of <link=puredaisy>Livingwood</link>, 3 blocks of <link=livingwood_stuff>Glimmering Livingwood</link>, one <link=a>Elven Gateway Core</link>, two <link=manapool>Mana Pools</link>, and two <link=pylon>Nature Pylons</link>. Build a frame of Livingwood, add the Glimmering Livingwood, place the core, set up the Mana Pools, and place the Nature Pylons on top of the pools.
lexicon_elfmania_alfheimgates_p2:
Crafting the Nature Pylon:










Crafting the Alfheim Core:
lexicon_elfmania_alfheimgates_p3:
1. <link=elvengatewaycore>Gateway Core to Alfheim</link>
2. <link=livingwood_stuff>Glimmering Livingwood</link>
3. <link=puredaisy>Livingwood</link>
4. <link=manapool>Mana Pool</link>
5. <link=pylon>Nature Pylon</link>
lexicon_elfmania_alfheimgates_p4:
To open the gateway, <link=nothing>right-click</link> the gateway core with the <link=wandoftheforest>Wand of the Forest</link>.

Opening requires a significant amount of mana stored in both pools. After activation, the portal will slowly consume mana to maintain its operation. If the mana is removed or runs out, the gateway will close. The mana cost to open it is many times higher than the upkeep cost, so closing the portal to save mana is not worthwhile.

The portal does not possess enough power to allow living beings through, though certain items may pass.

The amount of knowledge about Alfheim in this Lexicon will grow over time. Perhaps it would be wise to let the elves look upon forgotten things.
lexicon_elfmania_elfresources_p1:
<link=alfheim>Alfheim</link> holds many rare and exquisite resources. Most of them are difficult to obtain due to their high value and rivalry between elven clans. Nevertheless, the elves are willing to trade some materials for items from our world.

Among the most famous goods are <link=dreamwood>dreamwood</link>, <link=elementium>elementium</link>, <link=pixiedust>pixie dust</link> and <link=dragonstone>dragonstone</link>. Use the <link=alfheimgates>gateway to Alfheim</link> to establish trade and gain access to these treasures.
lexicon_elfmania_elfresources_p2:
Trading manasteel for elementium:


















A rare metal of the elves. Used for tools, armor, and advanced mechanisms.
lexicon_elfmania_elfresources_p3:
Trading mana gear for dragonstone:

















A rare crystal of Alfheim. Used for runic artifacts, armor, and powerful elven mechanisms.
lexicon_elfmania_elfresources_p4:
Trading Mana Glass for Elven Glass:

















Transparent glass from Alfheim. Looks beautiful, glows brightly, and is perfect for decorative builds.
lexicon_elfmania_elfresources_p5:
Trading Mana Quartz for Elven Dust:

















Magical Pixie Dust. Used in elven recipes and crafting special items.
lexicon_functional_flora_agricarnation_p: Slow plant growth often becomes a serious problem for any farm. The <link=agricarnation>Agricarnation</link> uses <link=mana>mana</link>, transforming it into a form of natural fertilizer that accelerates the growth of nearby crops and other plants. (I'll add growth acceleration mechanics for bushes and various trees a bit later.)
lexicon_functional_flora_hopperhock_p1:
The <link=hopperhock>Hopperhock</link> picks up items around itself and stores them in adjacent containers, such as <link=game:chest-east>chests</link>, <link=game:storagevessel-black-fired>vessels</link>, or practically anything capable of holding items. Supplying <link=mana>mana</link> is not required, but it increases the pickup radius.

If you don't want the Hopperhock to collect everything that ends up nearby, you can use <link=filterscroll>Filter Scrolls</link>.
lexicon_functional_flora_jadedamaranthus:
Everyone loves flowers, right? 
Then the <link=jadedamaranthus>Jaded Amaranthus</link> is made just for that.
Fed with <link=mana>mana</link> from a <link=manapool>mana pool</link>, it can quickly grow <link=mysticalflower>mystical flowers</link> in the area around it.
Is it that, or a clematis?

--Wait, you're about to summon a bunch of flowers in an instant?

--Yeah, so what?

--That's against the rules, you know?

--To hell with the rules, I have mana!!!
lexicon_functional_flora_puredaisy_p1: <link=puredaisy>Pure Daisy</link> is not only the very first flower, but also one of the most important flowers for any botanist. This flower purifies and transforms adjacent wood and stone blocks into their purified forms: <link=livingwood>Livingwood</link> and <link=puredaisy>Livingrock</link>, both of which are needed for crafting many items.
lexicon_functional_flora_puredaisy_p2: Just give it a minute or so...
lexicon_functional_flora_puredaisy_p4: <link=livingwood_firepit>Livingwood Firewood</link> can in turn be processed into <link=livingwood_stick>Livingwood Sticks.</link>
lexicon_functional_flora_puredaisy_p5:
Crafting the Pure Daisy.

















A blank sheet of paper for the beginning of your adventures.
lexicon_functional_flora_witheredamarant:
Not all magic has to be mysterious.
<link=witheredamaranthus>Withered Amaranthus</link>, feeding on <link=mana>mana</link> from a <link=manapool>mana pool</link>, can cause ordinary flowers around it to bloom rapidly.

Daisies, poppies, dandelions...
The strangest thing is that everyone looks “withered” except for it.

--You're spending mana... on ordinary flowers?

--What's wrong with ordinary flowers?

--Well, I expected something more magical.

--Sometimes that's more than enough, especially if you're an alchemist.
lexicon_generating_flora_daybloom_p1:
<link=daybloom>Daybloom</link> is the most basic but weak generating flower. It converts sunlight into mana, working on a principle similar to photosynthesis.

Daybloom works only under open sky in clear weather: at night, during rain, or under a roof it completely stops producing mana, and in winter its efficiency also drops greatly due to weak sunlight.

This is a very fragile plant and cannot withstand the flow of mana forever — exactly 24 in-game hours after planting, it completely withers and turns into a dead bush, so it should be treated as a consumable rather than a permanent generator.
lexicon_generating_flora_daybloom_p2:
The flower actively feeds from the soil, and the better the ground beneath it (medium fertility, high fertility, or terra preta), the faster it generates mana.

These flowers also dislike crowding: if planted too close to each other (closer than 7 blocks), they begin stealing light and nutrients from neighbors, sharply reducing the efficiency of all flowers in the area.
lexicon_generating_flora_endoflame_p1:
The <link=endoflame>Endoflame</link> is a generating flower that produces mana by burning fuel. It absorbs flammable items or blocks dropped nearby, one at a time, and generates mana until the fuel runs out.

Since the <link=endoflame>Endoflame</link> often becomes the first step toward automated mana generation, it is better not to rely on manual use alone. Because the flower requires dropped items, the <link=mechanical_dropper>Mechanical Dropper</link> works especially well with it. And if you find any other mechanism capable of supplying items, that will work perfectly too.
lexicon_generating_flora_generatingflora:
Generating mana should be one of the main priorities of any botanist. There are many <link=generatingflora>generating flowers</link> that can help with this. These flowers produce mana passively or actively depending on conditions.

All <link=category:generating_flora>Generating Flowers</link>, like <link=category:functional_flora>Functional Flowers</link>, have an internal mana buffer. To see the amount of mana, point the Wand of the Forest at the flower. Generated mana will be sent to the nearest <link=manaspreader>Mana Spreader</link>.

Generating flowers remember the Mana Spreader they were connected to. They will send mana only to it unless that spreader is removed.

To begin, it is recommended to use <link=endoflame>Endoflame</link> or <link=hydroangeas>Hydroangeas</link>. Endoflame uses fuel to generate mana, while Hydroangeas produces mana from water sources. If you prefer doing absolutely nothing and waiting, then <link=daybloom>Daybloom</link> or <link=nightshade>Nightshade</link> is perfect for you.
lexicon_generating_flora_rosaarcana_p1:
This flower only appears harmless, but in truth it hides immense power.
<link=rosaarcana>The Secret Rose</link> devours stability energy, transforming it into <link=mana>mana</link>. But in doing so, the flower weakens the boundary between the Rust World and the world of the Seraphim, allowing nightmares to slip into your reality.
lexicon_generating_flora_rosaarcana_p2: May Jonas protect those who dare use this flower.
lexicon_mana_management_catalyst_alchemy:
<link=mana>Mana</link> is an extremely ethereal substance whose properties are not yet fully understood. One of the few known uses is the creation of an <link=catalyst_alchemy>alchemical catalyst</link>. Placing it beneath a <link=manapool>mana pool</link> allows alchemical transformations to be performed directly within the pool.




Crafting the alchemical catalyst:

Transforming a stick into a root.











Transforming a root into a plank.

Transforming a plank into a beam.











Transforming a beam into a stick.

Converting rot into compost.











Converting small raw hide into regular leather.

Converting medium raw hide into regular leather.










Converting large raw hide into regular leather.

Converting huge raw hide into regular leather.










Converting bear raw hide into regular leather.

Converting compost into saltpeter.











Converting saltpeter into potash.

Converting onion into sulfur powder.











Converting cabbage into sulfur powder.

Converting potash into halite.











Converting charcoal into bituminous coal.
lexicon_mana_management_catalyst_conjura: An analogue of the <link=catalyst_alchemy>alchemical catalyst</link> also exists for <link=catalyst_conjuration>mana conjuration</link>. The <link=conjurationcatalyst>conjuration catalyst</link>, placed beneath a <link=manapool>mana pool</link>, allows blocks to be materialized directly from mana. The principle is similar to mana alchemy — simply throw items into the pool with the catalyst installed to trigger transformations.

Duplicating a stick:











Duplicating dry grass:

Duplicating iron oxide:











Duplicating a snowball:
lexicon_mana_management_manaintroduction:
Mana is a special ethereal substance. Simply put, it is a form of mystical energy. Its color may change depending on the surrounding environment. Mana management is one of the most important skills a botanist must master.

To obtain mana, you will need flowers from the <link=category:generating_flora>Generating Flora</link> category. Mana can be transferred using <link=manaspreader>Mana Spreaders</link> and stored in <link=manapool>Mana Pools</link>. It is used for a vast number of things, such as powering <link=category:functional_flora>Functional Flowers</link>.

To begin managing mana, it is recommended to study the use of <link=endoflame>Endoflame</link>, <link=hydroangeas>Hydroangeas</link>, <link=manaspreader>Mana Spreaders</link>, and <link=manapool>Mana Pools</link>.

Together they form a basic mana transfer system that can be used for many purposes. It is also recommended to review the <link=category:functional_flora>Functional Flora</link> and <link=naturalapparatus>Natural Apparatus</link> sections in this lexicon.
lexicon_mana_management_manapool_p1:
<link=manapool>Mana Pool</link> is essentially mana storage. Mana can be placed into the pool using <link=manaspreader>Mana Spreaders</link>. If a <link=manaspreader>spreader</link> is placed directly adjacent, it will automatically draw mana from the pool, increasing its internal buffer. All functional flowers require a nearby <link=manapool>Mana Pool</link> to draw energy from.

There are two types of <link=manapool>Mana Pools</link>: the <link=dilutedpool>Diluted Mana Pool</link>, capable of storing a small amount, and the <link=manapool>regular pool</link>, which stores 100 times more mana. When Mana Pool is mentioned, it usually refers to the regular version.
lexicon_mana_management_manapool_p2:
Crafting the <link=manapool>Mana Pool</link>.











Crafting the <link=dilutedpool>Diluted Mana Pool</link>.
lexicon_mana_management_manapool_p3:
<link=dilutedpool>Diluted Mana Pool</link> is useful when a small buffer is needed for a flower or a low-consumption system. A regular pool stores too much. Because of this, mana indicators in a regular pool may seem inaccurate, and the bar fills slowly simply because the storage is very large.

By throwing certain resources into a <link=manapool>Mana Pool</link>, you can infuse them with mana and turn them into stronger materials. Examples include <link=manasteel>Manasteel</link> and <link=manapearl>Mana Pearl</link>. The amount of mana can be checked the same way as with a <link=manaspreader>Mana Spreader</link>.

<link=manasteel>Manasteel</link> ingots, like other ingots, can be turned into other items using an anvil.
lexicon_mana_management_manapool_p5:
Creating <link=manapearl>Mana Gear</link>.











Infusing <link=game:flaxfibers>Flax Fiber</link> with mana.
lexicon_mana_management_manaspreader_p1:
<link=manaspreader>Mana Spreader</link> is the most important component in mana management. This block allows mana to move from point A to point B. If placed on the ground, it will face one of six directions.

The spreader can also be bound to a block using the <link=wandoftheforest>Wand of the Forest</link> by right-clicking the spreader and then right-clicking any block or Mana Pool.

<link=manaspreader>Mana Spreader</link> has a small internal mana buffer, which can be filled by nearby flowers from the <link=category:generating_flora>Generating Flora</link> category. This buffer can be viewed using the <link=wandoftheforest>Wand of the Forest</link> while looking at the spreader.
lexicon_mana_management_manaspreader_p2:
If the target can store mana, the spreader will release a mana burst that transfers energy.













Make sure Mana Pools always have space available so the spreader does not waste mana by sending it into already full pools.
lexicon_mana_management_manaspreader_p3: The spreader can release only one burst at a time. Until it reaches its target, no new one will appear. During flight, bursts gradually lose mana, which is visible by the shortening beam length.
lexicon_mana_management_manatablet_p1:
<link=manatablet>Mana Tablet</link> is portable mana storage. Unlike <link=manaspreader>Mana Spreaders</link>, it allows mana to be carried manually. Some items can directly use mana from the tablet, making it an important tool.

Throwing a <link=manatablet>Mana Tablet</link> into a <link=manapool>Mana Pool</link> allows mana to be transferred between them. Press <link=nothing>Shift+RMB</link> on the pool with the <link=wandoftheforest>Wand of the Forest</link> to change the transfer direction.
lexicon_mana_management_manatablet_p2: Unfortunately, it does not have Wi-Fi.
lexicon_mana_management_spark_p1:
<link=spark>Spark</link> is a rather curious object made of mana and raw energy. They can be placed on certain blocks that contain or store mana, including the <link=manapool>Mana Pool</link>. Some blocks can extract mana from storage through <link=spark>Sparks</link>, although early on their uses are limited. By placing one spark on a <link=manapool>Mana Pool</link> and another on a block that can use it, you allow remote access to mana, provided the distance is not too great.

To remove a <link=spark>Spark</link>, press <link=nothing>Shift+RMB</link> on it with the <link=wandoftheforest>Wand of the Forest</link>. Pressing simply <link=nothing>RMB</link> lets you see which sparks it can transfer mana to.
lexicon_mana_management_sparkaugment_p1:
<link=spark>Sparks</link> have more potential than it may seem. There are special augments that expand their functionality. They can only be installed into sparks placed above <link=manapool>Mana Pools</link>, and each spark can hold only one augment. <link=sparkdispersive>

Dispersive Augment</link> allows a spark to use mana from the pool to recharge items in the inventories of nearby players.

<link=sparkdominant>Dominant Augment</link> allows it to draw mana from neighboring pools without augments and transfer it into its own until full.
lexicon_mana_management_sparkaugment_p2:
<link=sparkrecessive>Recessive Augment</link> causes the spark to give mana from its own pool to others if their sparks have no augments or use dispersive augments. Transfer continues until the pool is completely emptied.

<link=sparkisolation>Isolating Augment</link> prevents interaction with dominant and recessive sparks, while still allowing mana transfer to devices through the spark network.
lexicon_mana_management_sparkaugment_p3:
Crafting the Isolating Augment:











Crafting the Dominant Augment:
lexicon_mana_management_sparkaugment_p4:
Crafting the Dispersive Augment:











Crafting the Recessive Augment:
lexicon_misc_flask_p1: A flask intended for collecting rusted air and water. Currently has no other uses.
lexicon_misc_livingrock_p1:
<link=livingrock>Livingrock</link> is one of the botanist's basic materials. It is used to create <link=manapool>Mana Pools</link> and various magical devices.

Beyond its practical uses, Livingrock is valued for its appearance and is well suited for constructing magical structures and decorative builds.

It can also be crafted into <link=livingrock_slab>Livingrock Slabs</link>, which are described in the corresponding article.
lexicon_misc_livingrock_p2: It only takes a little patience:
lexicon_misc_livingrock_slab_p1:
<link=livingrockslab>Livingrock Slabs</link> are most commonly used to create <link=dilutedpool>Diluted Mana Pools</link>.

However, they can also be used in construction — for example as neat pathways or decorative elements for botanical buildings.

Sometimes even the simplest materials find unexpected uses in the hands of an inventive botanist.
lexicon_misc_livingrock_slab_p2:
Crafting the Livingrock Slab:











Crafting the Diluted Mana Pool:
lexicon_misc_livingrock_stuff_p1: <link=livingrock>Livingrock</link> is a durable material created with the <link=puredaisy>Pure Daisy</link>. It serves as the foundation for many botanical structures and magical devices.

lexicon_misc_livingwood_firewood_p1: Livingwood Firewood is firewood obtained from Livingwood. Despite its magical origin, for now it has fairly simple uses. It can be used to craft Livingwood Sticks, which serve as the basis for various items and tools. In addition, Livingwood Firewood can be used as normal fuel — it works in furnaces, campfires, and can be charred into charcoal. At the moment it produces regular charcoal, but this may change in the future. If an interesting idea for special charcoal from Livingwood appears, it will certainly be added as Livingcoal.
lexicon_misc_livingwood_stick_p1: Livingwood Stick is a basic component made from Livingwood and infused with natural mana. It is most often used for crafting magical wands, but its uses do not end there. It is also used to create various tools, weapons, magical devices, and other natural mechanisms. Nearly any item related to Livingwood magic begins, in one way or another, with this simple but important component.
lexicon_misc_livingwood_stuff_p1:
<link=livingwood>Livingwood</link> is one of the main materials of Botania.











It is obtained using the <link=puredaisy>Pure Daisy</link> and is valued for its natural strength, ease of crafting, and beautiful appearance.

lexicon_misc_managlass_p1: <link=managlass>Managlass</link> is purified glass infused with mana. It combines the transparency of ordinary glass with a magical glowing effect and is often used in botanical structures and devices. Thanks to its purity, it conducts mana flows well through itself, allowing enclosed spaces where mana can pass through the Managlass.
lexicon_misc_root-normal_p1: There is no need to dig up tree roots if you have an <link=catalyst_alchemy>alchemy catalyst</link>. Simply throw a <link=game:stick>stick</link> into a <link=manapool>mana pool</link> placed on the catalyst, and it will transform into a <link=root>root</link>. This method is much easier and does not require harming nature.
lexicon_misc_root-normal_p2: Crafting a Root from a Stick:
lexicon_misc_root-rusted_p1:
Rust gradually consumes everything it touches and rarely brings anything good. However, it still has its uses. If you infuse a <link=root>tree root</link> with it, it will change and gain unusual properties.
Such a <link=rustyroot>rusty root</link> can be used in alchemy, the creation of certain flowers, and various mystical items. Its properties are not yet fully understood, but it is already clear — it is not just rusty junk.
lexicon_mystical_items_filterscroll_p1:
Black and White Scrolls allow you to configure item filtering in the <link=hopperhock>Hopperhock</link> and the <link=rustymirror>Rusty Mirror</link> (work in progress).

To open the filter configuration window, hold a scroll in your hand and press <link=nothing>Shift+Right Click</link>. After configuring it, insert the scroll into the Hopperhock using <link=nothing>Right Click</link>. The White Scroll allows you to specify which items will be accepted, while the Black Scroll defines items that will be excluded from filtering. Scrolls support both adding items through the interface and manually entering identifiers into a text field. For example, the entry "flower" will match all items containing that word in their ID, while "armor-body-plate" will match all plate chest armor regardless of the material they are made from.
lexicon_mystical_items_filterscroll_p2:
Crafting the White Scroll:











Crafting the Black Scroll:
lexicon_mystical_items_filterscroll_p3: Just in case you don't know how to find an ID: Interface Settings > Developer Mode > Debug Information
lexicon_mystical_items_floating_island_p:
A <link=floatingisland>Floating Island</link> is created from a <link=meadowseed>Meadow Seed</link>, any type of soil, and a <link=mysticalflower>Mystical Flower</link>. The island looks very unusual, emits a soft glow, and fits perfectly into magical decorative builds.

However, beauty is not its only advantage. <link=category:generating_flora>Generating Flora</link> and <link=category:functional_flora>Functional Flora</link> can be placed on the Floating Island while fully preserving their magical properties and functionality.

This makes the islands especially useful in complex magical structures where flowers need to be placed somewhere other than ordinary ground.

Crafting a Floating Island:










Planting a flower on the island:
lexicon_mystical_items_floralfertilizer_:
<link=floralfertilizer>Mystical Flower Fertilizer</link> is crafted from mystical flowers and is slightly more effective than ordinary fertilizer. However, that is not its greatest strength.
When applied to soil, it can cause various <link=mysticalflower>Mystical Flowers</link> to grow. This makes it a convenient way to obtain new flowers without having to search for them all over the world.
lexicon_mystical_items_flower_bag_p1: Due to the strange laws of this world, carrying large amounts of different flower types can be extremely inconvenient. Fortunately, the <link=flower_bag>Flower Bag</link> easily solves this problem. It can store up to one stack of every color of <link=mysticalflower>Mystical Flowers</link> and will also automatically collect them when they enter the player’s inventory.
lexicon_mystical_items_flower_bag_p2: To enable or disable auto-collection, press <link=nothing>Shift+RMB</link> in the air while holding the bag in your hand. In addition, by pressing <link=nothing>Shift+RMB</link> on a <link=game:chest-east>chest</link> or another inventory, you can quickly unload all flowers stored inside.
lexicon_mystical_items_manaitem_p1:
<link=manapool>Manasteel</link> is used to craft tools and armor. In terms of durability, it is comparable to copper: any metal ingot gains similar properties when infused with mana.

Its main difference is magic. When taking damage, manasteel items consume mana from available sources, such as a <link=manatablet>mana tablet</link>, instead of losing durability. This makes it a truly unique and indispensable material in the arsenal.



Crafting a manasteel plate:
lexicon_mystical_items_manaitem_p2:
A full armor set provides an additional bonus: reduces mana consumption of all magical items by 10%.

lexicon_mystical_items_meadowseed_p1:
By enriching <link=game:drygrass>grass</link> with <link=mana>mana</link>, a <link=meadowseed>Meadow Seed</link> can be created. It allows grass to spread quickly even in barren areas — simply use <link=nothing>RMB</link> on the desired block.

In addition to regular grass, the seed can also create other types of vegetation and soil, such as <link=game:peat-verysparse>peat</link> or <link=game:soil-medium-normal>medium fertility soil</link>.
lexicon_mystical_items_meadowseed_p2:
Meadow Seed for grass:

lexicon_mystical_items_meadowseed_p3: Meadow Seed for medium fertility soil:
lexicon_mystical_items_rod_of_the_seas_p:
<link=rod_of_the_seas>Rod of the Seas</link>, like its earthly counterpart, consumes <link=mana>mana</link> to create <link=game:water-still-7>water</link>. When used, it places a water block at the targeted location.
Additionally, the rod can be used to fill a <link=apothecary>petal apothecary</link>, <link=game:barrel>barrels</link>, <link=game:woodbucket>buckets</link>, and even a <link=game:claypot-black-fired>campfire pot</link>

Crafting the Rod of the Seas:










Keep the Earth clean — this is not Uranus.
lexicon_mystical_items_terrashatterer_p1:
The <link=terrashatterer>Terra Shatterer</link> is a tool capable of storing enormous amounts of <link=mana>mana</link> and converting it into destructive power. Its operating principle is similar to the <link=manatablet>Mana Tablet</link>: if you throw the pickaxe into a <link=manapool>Mana Pool</link>, it will begin charging gradually. However, the tool does not return the stored mana, so it cannot be used as a regular storage device.

The amount of stored mana determines the tool's <link=nothing>class</link>. There are six classes in total, from 0 to 5, with Class 0 being the base level. Each new class requires significantly more mana: advancing from 0 to 1 takes about a minute, while the cost of every subsequent level increases by roughly ten times up to Class 5.
lexicon_mystical_items_terrashatterer_p2:
The class does not affect mining speed, but instead enhances the <link=nothing>active ability</link>, which is activated by pressing <link=nothing>Right Click</link>. While active, the tool begins destroying blocks in an area: at Class 2 it affects a small column, at Class 3 a 3x3 area, and beyond that the radius continues to grow. Class 0 tools do not have this ability. While the ability is active, it gradually consumes mana even if the pickaxe is not being used.

Like items made from <link=manaitem>Manasteel</link>, the Terra Shatterer consumes mana instead of normal durability. The higher its class, the more mana is required to maintain its operation.
lexicon_mystical_items_terrashatterer_p3:

Crafting the Terra Shatterer:
lexicon_mystical_items_terrasteelitem_p1:
<link=manapool>Terrasteel</link> is used to create durable and reliable tools and armor. Its properties are comparable to iron, making it suitable for combat and everyday tasks.

Its key feature is mana interaction. When taking damage, items consume mana from available sources, such as a <link=manatablet>mana tablet</link>, preventing durability loss. Equipment does not wear down as long as mana is available.



Crafting a Terrasteel plate:

lexicon_mystical_items_wandofbinding_p1:
<link=wandofbindings>Wand of Mass Binding</link> is crafted from <link=manapool>Manasteel</link> and a <link=livingwood_stick>Livingwood Twig</link>. It allows you to bind several nearby flowers at once to a <link=manaspreader>Mana Spreader</link> or <link=manapool>Mana Pool</link>.

Press <link=nothing>RMB</link> on the desired block to automatically bind nearby flowers to it.
lexicon_mystical_items_wandofbinding_p2:
A wand for the lazy?:










Crafting the Binding Staff Core:
lexicon_natural_apparatus_hourglass_p1:
The <link=hourglass>Floating Hourglass</link> is a mystical device capable of influencing the operation of certain mechanisms. The hourglass intercepts their behavior and activates the connected device only at the moment when all sand has completely fallen to the bottom.

If there is no sand inside the hourglass, the linked mechanism will be blocked and unable to function. To set the time, place between 1 and 64 units of <link=game:sand-granite>sand</link> inside the hourglass. Each unit of sand corresponds to one second.

At the moment, it is confirmed that the hourglass can control the <link=mechanical_dropper>Mechanical Dropper</link>, allowing precise adjustment of item ejection intervals. Simply place the hourglass directly next to the dropper.
lexicon_natural_apparatus_mechanical_dro:
The <link=mechanical_dropper>Mechanical Dropper</link> is a simple device used for automatically ejecting items. It must be connected to the <link=game:woodenaxle-ud>axle</link> of a <link=game:sail>windmill</link> or a <link=game:waterwheel-3m-north>water wheel</link>, after which it will begin ejecting items from its internal storage roughly once every 5 seconds.

An <link=hourglass>Hourglass</link> can be placed next to the dropper. In that case, the device will follow the hourglass interval and eject items with the specified delay. If all sand is removed from the hourglass, the dropper will stop working.
This mechanism is especially useful for automatically supplying items to mana generators, flowers, or various alchemical constructions.
lexicon_rusted_world_artifacts_blackhole:
The Void is a vast space filled with... emptiness. There is absolutely nothing inside it. However, with enough ingenuity, this space can be used to store an unlimited number of items.
<link=blackholetalisman>The Black Hole Talisman</link> uses the powerful magic of Gaia and the Rusted World, allowing it to store practically infinite amounts of a single item type in one slot. The talisman is capable of storing nearly any items and blocks.

To bind an item or block to the talisman, simply drag it with your mouse into an empty talisman. Blocks also support an alternative binding method: hold an empty talisman in your hand and press <link=nothing>Shift+Right Click</link> on the desired block.

Pressing <link=nothing>Shift+Right Click</link> in the air will toggle the talisman on or off.
While active and present in your inventory, it will automatically absorb items of the selected type.

To place an item inside the talisman, drag it onto the talisman and press <link=nothing>Left Click</link>, or use the talisman itself on the item with <link=nothing>Left Click</link>.

To extract items, press <link=nothing>Right Click</link> on an empty slot to extract a single item, or <link=nothing>Ctrl+Right Click</link> on an empty slot to extract an entire stack.

All items from the talisman can be quickly transferred into a chest or other container by pressing <link=nothing>Shift+Right Click</link> on the container.

Crafting the Talisman Frame:



Crafting the Black Hole Talisman:
lexicon_rusted_world_artifacts_flight-ti:
<link=flight-tiara>Winged Tiara</link> — an artifact inspired by the ancient race of the Flugel. Once, they were the dominant species, created as god-slayers and possessing the ability to fly. With the help of <link=gaiaspirit>Gaia Spirits</link>, part of their power has been recreated: the tiara allows flight at the cost of mana.

However, the technology is imperfect. The tiara cannot sustain flight indefinitely — if used for more than 60 seconds without a break, it will stop working until it recovers. A flight meter on the screen is used for control. In addition to flight, the tiara grants the ability to dash and glide. Pressing the sprint key during flight performs a dash in the direction you are looking. Dashes can be used once every 2 seconds.

Gliding allows you to descend smoothly, travel horizontally, and avoid fall damage. To do this, crouch while falling. Even with an empty flight meter, you can glide, though regeneration will be slower.




Crafting the tiara frame:



Crafting the Winged Tiara:
lexicon_rusted_world_artifacts_rustworld:
<link=enderair>Vial of Rusted World Air</link> contains distorted air collected from rifts leading to an alien world. To fill the vial, hold an <link=flask>empty vial</link> in your hand and press <link=nothing>RMB</link> near a rift.

This air possesses mutating properties, though their nature remains unstudied.
It is only known that it can be used in the creation of certain mystical items.
